Title: Selangor Sultan backs King on Rukun Negara decree
Article:
SHAH ALAM: The Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah, backed His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, in his
decree
against those who reject the principles of the Rukun Negara.
In conjunction with the King's official birthday, Sultan Sharafuddin and the Tengku Permaisuri of Selangor, Tengku Permaisuri Norashikin, extended their congratulations to His Majesty today.
In a post on the Selangor Royal Office Facebook page, Sultan Sharafuddin also prayed that Sultan Ibrahim and Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia, be blessed with continued good health, a long life filled with blessings, and remain under the protection and mercy of Allah as they continue to safeguard the nation and lead the people with wisdom and authority.
"The Sultan of Selangor also supports and welcomes His Majesty's decree that any person who rejects the principles of the Rukun Negara is not worthy to be called a Malaysian citizen," the post said.
Sultan Sharafuddin said the people, especially the younger generation, must understand, appreciate and practise the values enshrined in the Rukun Negara to build a harmonious and peaceful society among Malaysia's multi-ethnic population.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Op Tiris: Cops seize five vehicles believed modified with extra fuel tanks
Article:
KANGAR: Police have seized five vehicles suspected of being modified with additional fuel tanks concealed beneath the rear seats at separate locations around the Padang Besar district.
Padang Besar police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Shokri Abdullah said the seizure of one pickup truck and four sedan cars was carried out under Op Tiris 4.0 between April 22 until today.
He said a local woman, two local men and two foreign men aged between 30 and 55 were detained during the operation.
"The three locally-registered vehicles and two foreign-registered vehicles seized in the operation were taken to the Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department for further action and have been impounded for investigation purposes.
"The total value of the seizure is estimated at RM36,300," he said in a statement today.
He said police would continue to intensify integrated enforcement operations to curb the smuggling of subsidised fuel and all forms of cross-border crime that could threaten public safety and order.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: France seizes Russia-linked oil tanker with ties to Iranian magnate
Article:
BREST, France: France said on Monday it had detained a Russia-linked oil tanker in the Atlantic, with the Kremlin comparing the latest seizure aimed at tackling Moscow's sanctions-busting shadow fleet to "international piracy."
Since September, France has boarded three other ships believed to belong to the shadow fleet, used by Russia to circumvent Western sanctions on its fossil fuel exports imposed over the Kremlin's 2022 invasion of Ukraine.
The Tagor was detained on Sunday morning in international waters with the help of Britain and other partners, after its Russian captain refused to comply with orders, prosecutors said.
President Emmanuel Macron posted a video of the seizure, which showed commandos rappelling from a helicopter onto the ship.
"We consider these acts illegal. They border on international piracy," said President Vladimir Putin's spokesman, Dmitry Peskov.
"Russia is taking measures to ensure the safety of its cargo."
Suspected of carrying Russian or Iranian oil despite international sanctions, the Tagor is linked to petroleum shipping magnate Mohammad Hossein Shamkhani, according to open-source database Opensanctions.org.
According to French authorities, the Tagor was on its way from Murmansk in northwestern Russia when it was boarded.
The ship was falsely flying a Cameroonian flag and was heading toward Limbe, a seaside city in the west of the African country, said a spokesperson for France's Atlantic maritime prefecture.
Shadow fleet vessels frequently change the flags they fly, a practice known as flag-hopping, or use invalid registrations in an attempt to escape tracking.
"It is unacceptable for ships to circumvent international sanctions, violate the law of the sea, and fund the war that Russia has been waging against Ukraine for more than four years," Macron said on X.
'STEPPING UP RESPONSES'
The UK ministry of defence said a British helicopter had "provided tracking and monitoring in support of the French operation to board the tanker Tagor."
"We are stepping up our response to shadow vessels – to choke off the funds that fuel Putin's illegal invasion of Ukraine," a ministry spokesperson said.
The Atlantic maritime prefecture said the interception had taken place more than 400 nautical miles (740 kilometres) west of Brittany.
"The examination of the documents confirmed doubts about the irregularity of the flag being flown," the prefecture said.
The ship, which had 23 crew members, was "being escorted by the French navy to an anchorage point for further checks", the prefecture said.
When asked about the tanker's ties to Shamkhani, officials declined to comment.
Shamkhani is the son of security official Ali Shamkhani, who was an adviser to the former Iranian supreme leader Ali Khamenei, both of whom were killed on February 28, the first day of the US-Israeli attacks that started the Middle East war.
BOARDING 'PROVED NECESSARY'
The Russian embassy in France said it had requested information about the makeup of the crew.
"No notifications have been provided by the French side regarding actions taken against this vessel," the embassy said.
The tanker, which is under EU and US sanctions, has flown the flags of Madagascar, the Marshall Islands and Panama.
Prosecutors said that "taking control of the vessel proved necessary."
The prosecutor's office in the northwestern city of Brest said a criminal investigation had been opened over failure to prove a vessel's nationality, absence of a flag and refusal to comply.
"It is a vessel that was known and tracked," Guillaume Le Rasle, spokesman for the Atlantic maritime prefecture, told AFP.
"The objective of the diversion is to verify the validity of its flag," he added.
The three other ships France has detained since September on suspicion of belonging to the shadow fleet were allowed to sail after their owners paid fines.
In April, France announced a plan to double penalties for ships that fail to fly a flag or refuse to comply.
Several Western countries have imposed sanctions on hundreds of vessels in Russia's shadow fleet over its 2022 invasion of Ukraine.
Nearly 600 ships suspected of belonging to the fleet are subject to European Union sanctions. - AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: One missing, six saved after boat capsizes in Kapit
Article:
SIBU: A man is feared drowned after the boat he was on capsized in Sungai Gaat, Kapit, near SK Nanga Balang yesterday evening.
A Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department operations centre spokesman said the victim, Spielberg Dylan Sekunyir, 32, is missing while six other passengers who were on the boat managed to save themselves.
"Based on information from the victim's friend, the boat capsized about 6.15pm when it hit a rock in the middle of the river.
"The six individuals managed to save themselves while the victim was seen drifting about 50m from where the boat capsized before disappearing from view," the spokesman said in a statement today, adding that the victim has yet to be found.
The department received a call at 9.26am today and a team of firefighters arrived at 10.42am to conduct a search and rescue operation in a 6km radius from where the victim was last seen.
The search was suspended at 3.30pm due to rainy weather.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor Amanah confident voters will back state govt aligned with Putrajaya
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Johor Amanah has signalled its readiness for the state election, declaring that the Pakatan Harapan (PH) machinery has already been activated following the dissolution of the state assembly today.
Johor Amanah chairman Aminolhuda Hassan said
Johor PH was acting in line with resolutions
made at the recent PH convention, where preparations for the state polls had already been set in motion.
"Johor PH, as stated at the recent convention, has already mobilised its machinery and is prepared to face the state election," he said.
Aminolhuda added that Johor Amanah was confident voters would make a considered choice in electing a state government aligned with the federal government led by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, who is also PH chairman.
"Johor Amanah believes the people of Johor will make the best decision in selecting a state government in line with the unity government," he told the New Straits Times.
The Johor state election is expected to see a tightly contested race marked by multi-cornered battles involving Barisan Nasional, PH, Perikatan Nasional, Bersama and other emerging political blocs.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: EC to discuss key dates for Johor polls after official notification of dissolution
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Election Commission will hold a special meeting for the 16th Johor election as soon as it receives official notification from the speaker of the assembly on the date of the dissolution.
EC chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun said the special meeting would discuss the key dates for the state polls.
"The EC takes note of the press conference by Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi regarding the dissolution of the 15th Johor legislative assembly (today).
"As such, the EC will announce a date to hold a special meeting to discuss the key dates for the 16th Johor election as soon as it receives official notification from the speaker on the date of the dissolution," he said.
Earlier today, Onn Hafiz, at a special press conference at his official residence in Saujana, announced the
dissolution of the state assembly
, paving the way for a fresh state election.
He said the decision followed the consent of the Regent of Johor, Tunku Mahkota Ismail, during an audience earlier.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: News@9: Today's top headlines - June 1, 2026 [WATCH]
Article:
HERE are today's biggest stories:
King urges restraint
His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, has reminded political leaders to exercise caution in their public statements and uphold proper conduct.
Johor polls loom

Social media deadline
The MCMC has given users under 16 one month to transfer their data before account restrictions and suspensions take effect.
Ee Wei injured
Mixed doubles world champion Toh Ee Wei has suffered a partial ACL tear, putting her World Championships title defence in doubt.
That's all for News@9.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: Stay clear of provocation, divisive politics, Samsuri tells PN
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Perikatan Nasional chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar has called on the coalition's leadership, machinery and party members to stay clear of provocation, hate politics and divisive tactics during the Johor election.
He said PN would enter the election arena with responsibility, maturity and professionalism, with the interests of the people as its priority.
"I hope the entire machinery can avoid any form of extreme provocation, hate politics and divisiveness, and instead present a calmer, fact-based and solution-oriented politics," he said in a statement.
Samsuri, who is also Pas vice-president, said the election was not merely a political contest but a platform to offer better solutions to the people, particularly on issues of cost of living, job opportunities, state economic development, administrative integrity and the future of the nation.
He called on all component party leaders, machinery and members to give their full focus to election preparations with a spirit of togetherness, discipline and unity of action.
"InsyaAllah, with the cooperation of all parties and the support of the people, Perikatan Nasional will continue to bring a stable, clean and caring governance agenda for a better future for Johor and Malaysia," he said.
Earlier today, Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi
announced the dissolution
of the Johor state assembly, paving the way for the next state election to be held within 60 days.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Health Ministry moves to block 4 online vape shops
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Health Ministry has submitted requests to the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) to block four websites for online advertising and sale of vape products.
In a statement, it said that the four websites are part of 10 sites suspected of violating the Control of Smoking Products for Public Health Act.
"Investigations into the remaining six are still ongoing," it said, adding that their requests were made under Section 263(2) of the Communications and Multimedia Act.
The Control of Smoking Products for Public Health Act came into force on Oct 1, 2024, with full enforcement beginning Oct 1 last year.
"As of April 30, a total of 2,595 investigation papers have been opened involving various offences under Sections 7, 8, 9 and 10 of the Act."
The ministry also said it has carried out 34,903 enforcement operations nationwide, inspecting 683,704 premises.
"A total of 233,831 compound notices were issued for compoundable offences, while 4,226 investigation papers were opened for non-compoundable offences.
"For court action, 398 cases are still ongoing, while 204 cases have been settled, resulting in fines totalling RM1,189,100."
The ministry said it would continue to monitor online advertising and promotion of tobacco products, including vape sales.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Election fever builds in Johor as Khaled Nordin steps up ground engagement
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Election fever is already rippling through Johor's political landscape just hours after the announcement of the dissolution of the state assembly.
Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Khaled Nordin, who is Kota Tinggi member of parliament, is among those expected to intensify ground outreach at his constituency as political activity begins to shift into pre-campaign mode.
He is set to continue his constituency-focused outreach with a series of public engagements tomorrow.
His programme begins with a morning tea session with the Chinese community at Laughing Canteen Kopitiam at 8.45am, followed by a informal dialogue session with taxi and e-hailing drivers at the Tun Sri Lanang Taxi Terminal, here, at 10am.
The sessions are jointly organised by the Kota Tinggi parliamentary service centre and Umno Kota Tinggi division.
While Khaled's engagement is officially a community engagement, the timing of the programme has added to the sense that political parties are already shifting into campaign mode, with ground sentiment expected to play a decisive role in the upcoming contest.
The 16th Johor Election is expected to see largely four-cornered contests, with Barisan Nasional, Pakatan Harapan, Perikatan Nasional and Bersama all set to vie for the 56 state seats.
Most constituencies are tipped to feature multi-cornered battles, with some potentially turning into more crowded fights if smaller parties and independents join the race.
The polls are being closely watched as a key barometer of support for the BN-led state government, the unity administration, and the strength of the opposition ahead of the next general election.
The
state assembly was dissolved
today, triggering preparations for the next state election and effectively resetting the political board in Johor.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Maharani Freeport group exec chairman conferred 'Tan Sri' title
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Maharani Freeport Sdn Bhd group executive chairman Tan Sri Dr Daing A Malek Daing A Rahaman has been conferred the Darjah Kebesaran Panglima Mangku Negara (PMN), which carries the title "Tan Sri", by His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia.
The honour was conferred in conjunction with His Majesty's official birthday celebration today.
An advisory member of the Johor Council of Royal Court, Daing A Malek, who is Country Garden Pacificview Sdn Bhd executive director, is also the executive chairman of Maharani Freeport's parent entity, Maharani Energy Gateway Sdn Bhd.
He received his undergraduate degree from Universiti Teknologi Malaysia (UTM) in 1979. In November 2023, he was awarded an Honorary Doctorate of Management from UTM.
He is the founder of Astaka Holdings Ltd, founded in 1993, and has held executive chairman positions at Astaka Padu Sdn Bhd, a chairman position at SIPP Energy Sdn Bhd, and a director position at Seaview Holdings Sdn Bhd.
His former positions include executive vice chairman at Damansara Holdings Bhd from 2014 to 2018 and non-executive director at Knusford Bhd from 2013 to 2015.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BN-led Johor govt has a proven track record, says Wee
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: MCA president Datuk Seri Dr Wee Ka Siong said that the Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) government proved that political stability, effective governance, and a clear development agenda could yield beneficial results for the people.
He said this following the
dissolution of the Johor state assembly
today, adding that it will pave the way for a fresh mandate from the people.
"From investment records and job creation to welfare assistance and infrastructural development, Johor has continued to move forward excellently.
"The dissolution of the state assembly marks another significant chapter in Johor's democratic journey," he said in a statement today.
The 56-seat assembly was officially dissolved today to pave the way for the 16th state election.
The dissolution was announced by Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi during a special press conference at his official residence.
Wee, who is also the Ayer Hitam member of parliament, is confident that Johor voters would make wise choices based on performance, vision, and commitment to the state's future.
"As we prepare for this state election, let us uphold the spirit of democracy, mutual respect, and solidarity, while working together towards a more prosperous and progressive Johor," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Explosion kills 5 at South Korean aerospace plant
Article:
SEOUL: An explosion at a plant run by South Korean defence firm Hanwha Aerospace killed five people on Monday, local fire authorities said.
The explosion was reported to authorities at around 11:00 am (0200 GMT) at Hanwha's plant in Daejeon, about 150 kilometres south of Seoul.
A representative of the Daejeon Fire Headquarters told AFP that five people were killed and two others injured, without providing further details.
Hanwha Aerospace is a major South Korean defence manufacturer which produces weapons, artillery systems and aerospace components.
Its Daejeon facility focuses on research and development, including advanced weapons technologies and space-related systems.
The blast may have happened during the cleaning process involved in the production of rocket propellants, Hanwha Aerospace CEO Son Jae-il said at a news briefing.
"During this process, explosive materials could remain on the tools, and there is a procedure to remove and clean those residues. It is presumed that the accident occurred during this stage of the process," he said.
"We will take this accident with the utmost seriousness and fundamentally review and rectify the company's safety systems to ensure that such an incident is never repeated."
Major political parties will refrain from using campaign songs and choreographed performances, as a sign of respect, during stumping ahead of Wednesday's local elections.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hassan Karim: Hold Johor polls and GE16 together for political stability
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Pasir Gudang member of parliament Hassan Abdul Karim has welcomed the dissolution of the Johor assembly as a move that will reduce political uncertainty.
However, for the same reason, he said Parliament should be dissolved as well to pave the way for the 16th General Election (GE16) to be held simultaneously with the Johor Election.
"The dissolution of the Johor assembly helps reduce tension and political guesswork — at least in Johor there is now certainty.
"The sooner the state election and GE16 are held, and if held simultaneously, the better it is for creating political stability in the country, so that after the state and general elections, the state and federal governments can focus on the development agenda," he told the
New Straits Times
.
However, the veteran PKR lawmaker said he believed it would be unlikely that Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim would dissolve Parliament so soon.
Meanwhile, Bersatu secretary-general Datuk Seri Azmin Ali said the party had already begun its preparations for the Johor Election, with its central political bureau holding a more than three-hour engagement session with the Johor leadership this morning.
He said the session, chaired by party president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin, identified key target areas, voter sentiment, winning strategies and local issues ahead of the polls.
"Bersatu is confident that preparations this time are more organised and the development of a strategic plan will help secure victory in Johor," he said.
Earlier today, Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi
announced the dissolution
of the Johor assembly, paving the way for the next state election to be held within 60 days.
The last state election was held on March 12, 2022, called early by then-menteri besar Datuk Seri Hasni Mohammad when he dissolved the state assembly on Jan 22.
Barisan Nasional managed a landslide victory, capturing 40 out of 56 state seats.
Pakatan Harapan won 12 seats while Perikatan Nasional secured three seats and Muda one.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia has the right to demand compensation from Norway, says Khaled
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is actively pursuing alternative solutions to meet its defence requirements following the cancellation of the naval strike missile (NSM) procurement, but will continue seeking accountability and compensation from Norway over the failed deal.
Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said moving on from the cancelled procurement did not mean accepting what had happened without consequences.
Khaled was responding to a New Straits Times article, titled "Move on from missile deal and find replacement," where defence
analyst Zaki Salleh
had called for the government to formally close the NSM procurement chapter and pursue legal action to recover its losses.
Zaki had said Norway was unlikely to reinstate the export licence for the NSM procurement despite apologising to Malaysia in a meeting at the International Institute for Strategic Studies Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore.
Khaled, meanwhile, said Malaysia had every right to seek appropriate compensation from Norway and the company involved, noting that a substantial amount of taxpayers' money had already been committed to the programme.
"Yes, Malaysia is moving on. In fact, we are actively pursuing alternative solutions to meet our defence requirements.
"However, moving on does not mean forgetting or accepting without accountability," Khaled said in a statement.
He said the country had suffered not only financial losses but also the loss of valuable time and opportunities after Norway cancelled the export licence for the missile system.
"If Norway had the courtesy of informing us earlier about the export licence cancellation, those funds could have been utilised elsewhere.
"Instead, Malaysia has suffered not only financial losses, but also the loss of valuable time and opportunities."
Khaled said replacing the NSM system would not be a straightforward process as systems of such sophistication could not simply be swapped out overnight.
He said identifying a suitable alternative, negotiating new contracts and integrating a replacement into existing platforms would require considerable time and resources.
The minister added that the consequences of the cancellation extended well beyond immediate financial losses.
"More importantly, this episode goes beyond a bilateral contractual dispute. It has placed Malaysia's national defence and security in a compromised position."
Khaled said there must be a clear message to Norway and the broader international defence community that such conduct could not pass without consequence.
"If breaking defence commitments carries no meaningful cost, the problem will repeat itself. Norway must be held to account in a manner that serves as a genuine deterrent, not offered an easy path to reconciliation."
He also expressed concern over what he described as a troubling trend where commitments and contracts could be overturned by political or ideological considerations, weakening trust in international agreements.
Khaled said smaller and middle powers depended on a predictable international environment where obligations were honoured and contracts fulfilled.
"If states and companies can walk away from commitments without accountability, it sets a dangerous precedent for future defence, economic and strategic cooperation."
He said compensation was not merely about recovering money but also about ensuring accountability for the broader consequences arising from the cancellation.
"If Norway claims to respect the principles of the rules-based international order, and value its longstanding relationship with Malaysia, it should support a fair and reasonable compensation process that reflects the full consequences of this cancellation."
Khaled added that while Malaysia was moving forward with its defence plans, Norway still owed Malaysians accountability and transparency over the episode.
"The bilateral relationship is now defined by deep mistrust, and things cannot simply continue as before. Moving forward should not come at the expense of the principles that underpin responsible international conduct."
In April,
Norway withdrew several export licences
related to certain technologies as part of efforts to tighten the country's export controls, which affected the NSM procurement agreement between the Royal Malaysian Navy and the manufacturer of the NSM system.
Following that, Malaysia issued a notice of demand to a
Norwegian defence firm
over the cancellation of an export licence for the NSM missile system intended for the Littoral Combat Ship and Lekiu-class frigates, claiming losses exceeding RM1 billion.
The government is seeking both direct and indirect costs following Norway's unilateral decision to revoke the export licence for the defence system.
Malaysia had already paid €126 million (RM583 million), equivalent to 95 per cent of the contract value, before the cancellation.
, military procurement, Malaysia news
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: I'm not jumping ship, says DAP's Marina
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: DAP assemblyman Marina Ibrahim has denied claims that she is defecting or has been "bought over" following her decision not to defend her Skudai seat in the upcoming Johor state election.
"Some have said, 'Are you jumping parties?' or 'Have you been bought over?' No.
"I have explained that every decision I make is made after discussions with those who have been my backbone throughout this journey. They are the people who understand how I work," she said in a Facebook post.
She added that many have begun forming negative perceptions and assumptions simply because she is not vocal in her criticism.
Marina cited the teachings of Chinese strategist Sun Tzu, saying that his work is often misunderstood.
"Many people think Sun Tzu teaches how to defeat enemies in war. In reality, he teaches how to achieve objectives while minimising conflict," she said.
Marina said as a politician, she does not believe in opposing everything, solely because of political differences.
"I have always believed that politics is about solving problems on the ground."
She added that Sun Tzu also taught that a leader must understand the situation before acting.
"In Johor, I am not part of the state government.
But not everything needs confrontation. Many issues can be solved through good working relations, mature negotiation and building bridges between parties.
"That is why I may seem less politically active when the time is not right. My priority is always the people on the ground."
Marina added that politics has its time, especially during elections, but helping the people cannot wait for a season.
"A politician's strength is not measured by conflict, but by how many people's problems are solved.
"That is the spirit I uphold in service; winning without fighting, through relationships and negotiation."
In Johor's context, Marina said it also aligns with a culture that values stability, cooperation, and practical solutions over prolonged confrontation.
"You may say I am weak, soft. It doesn't matter. People's views and interpretations vary.
"But to me, I see it as strategic diplomacy to achieve more for my constituency."
Yesterday, Marina, who is serving her first term, announced that s
he will not defend her seat
in the next Johor election.
She declined to confirm the authenticity of a letter purportedly rejecting an offer to contest the Tiram state seat and a chairmanship in a government-linked company.
The
viral letter
, dated May 30, purportedly showed Marina rejecting an offer to leave Skudai and contest the Tiram state seat, as well as an alleged proposal for her to be appointed to a government-linked entity should she fail to win.
She said she intends to return to community work, describing grassroots service as a calling that has always been close to her heart.
In the 2022 Johor election, Marina won the Skudai seat for Pakatan Harapan with a majority of more than 13,000 votes against candidates from Barisan Nasional and Perikatan Nasional.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'No easy fight, but we are ready,' says PH as Johor heads to polls
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Johor Pakatan Harapan said it was fully prepared for the state's 16th election after the legislative assembly was dissolved today.
State Amanah and PH chairman Aminolhuda Hassan, Johor PKR chairman Datuk Seri Dr Zaliha Mustafa and state DAP chairman Teo Nie Ching said they had anticipated that Barisan Nasional would seek a fresh mandate in the near future and maintained that preparations for the election had been underway.
They said the previously announced state assembly sitting scheduled for June 22 had appeared increasingly unlikely in light of political developments.
[[nid:1453128]]
"From the beginning, PH anticipated that BN would dissolve the state assembly in the near future. It has now become clear that the assembly sitting announced for June 22 was unrealistic," they said in a joint statement.
PH said it would continue with existing election preparations and elevate discussions to the coalition's national presidential council to determine the next course of action.
The coalition stressed that it was approaching the coming election with caution despite the challenges ahead.
"For us, there is no easy election. PH is ready to mobilise all efforts to secure victory in this state election."
Earlier today, Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi announced that the 56-seat state assembly had been
dissolved with immediate effect
, paving the way for fresh elections.
The state election must be held within 60 days.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Former MAG chief Izham Ismail joins Philippine Airlines board
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Former Malaysia Aviation Group (MAG) managing director Datuk Captain Izham Ismail has been appointed as an independent director of Philippine Airlines (PAL), marking his latest move in the aviation industry following his retirement from MAG earlier this year.
According to the Philippine-based business publication insider Insider PH, PAL has appointed Izham and fuel industry veteran Edgar Chua to its board as independent directors.
The appointments come as the Philippine flag carrier continues to strengthen its leadership team and support its long-term growth strategy.
PAL chairman and chief executive officer (CEO) Lucio C. Tan welcomed the appointments saying both directors would bring valuable experience to the airline.
"We are delighted to welcome Chua and Izham to PAL's board of directors."
"Chua's extensive business leadership experience and Izham's global aviation expertise will be invaluable as PAL continues to pursue sustainable growth, strengthen governance and expand its role in connecting the Philippines to the world," Tan said, as reported by Insider PH.
Business Times has contacted PAL for comment.
PAL is going through restructuring of its own with broader leadership changes in the airline.
In April 2025, the airline named former SriLankan Airlines CEO Richard Nuttall as its new president effective May 29 last year.
PAL said in its statement that the move was part of its efforts to strengthen its management team and support its international growth ambitions.
Izham retired as MAG group managing director on Jan 31 this year after more than four decades with Malaysia Airlines and its parent group.
He led the airline through the Covid-19 pandemic and played a key role in its restructuring and post-pandemic recovery.
Izham joined Malaysia Airlines as a cadet pilot in 1979 and later held several senior management positions before being appointed as MAG group managing director in December 2017.
PAL's decision to bring in experienced business leader and airline executive comes as the airline continues to deliver strong financial performance while advancing its fleet renewal plans.
In its latest financial results, PAL reported a net income of RM311 million (US$78.55 million) for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026, up 2.6 per cent from the same period a year earlier.
Total revenue rose 9.7 per cent year-on-year to RM3.5 billion (US$895.70 million) for driven by sustained passenger demand, stronger cargo yields and growth in ancillary revenue streams.
Passenger revenue increased 8.7 per cent toRM3 billion (US$759.65 million) as the airline carried 4.3 million passengers during the quarter while cargo revenue climbed 22.5 per cent to RM171 million (US$43.21 million).
PAL received its first Airbus A350-1000 aircraft in Dec 2025 becoming the first carrier in Southeast Asia to operate the aircraft type.
On May 29, 2026 the airline received its second A350-1000 aircraft.
It remains the first and only carrier in the region to operate the A350-1000 aircraft.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pas urges simultaneous elections in other states, GE16 alongside Johor
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Pas has called for the dissolution of Parliament and other state assemblies so that elections can be held simultaneously with Johor.
Its deputy president, Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man, said polls in Melaka, Negri Sembilan, Sarawak and Perlis should be held at the same time as the Johor Election so that time and resources can be saved.
"It would be even better if the general election were held at the same time," he told the
New Straits Times
.
His comments come following the
dissolution of the Johor assembly
, announced by Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Onn Hafiz Ghazi earlier today.
Tuan Ibrahim said Pas would convene an emergency meeting to discuss the Johor Election, and that Perikatan Nasional was also expected to hold a meeting in the near future.
He said the party would hear preliminary assessments from its central and state election departments, particularly on the number of seats and candidates to be fielded.
He said, while Johor Pas has already identified several of its traditional seats in Johor to contest, it has yet to finalise the total number of constituencies where it will field candidates.
The final decision, he said, would be made at the upcoming PN meeting.
The last state election was held on March 12, 2022, called early by then-menteri besar Datuk Seri Hasni Mohammad following the dissolution of the state assembly on Jan 22.
Barisan Nasional won a landslide victory, capturing 40 out of 56 state seats.
Pakatan Harapan won 12 seats while PN secured three seats and Muda one.
The Johor assembly would have automatically dissolved in April next year if elections were not called earlier.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysian troops complete multinational military exercise in India
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysian troops enhanced their interoperability with regional forces during a multinational military exercise hosted by the Indian army in Meghalaya, India.
The 10-member Malaysian army contingent comprising two officers and eight personnel was among more than 400 troops who took part in the exercise.
The exercise, held at Umroi Military Station in Meghalaya from May 18 to 31, concluded with a 72-hour validation exercise that demonstrated the interoperability, mutual trust and collective readiness of participating armed forces in addressing common security challenges.
[[nid:1453107]]
Participating nations also include India, Bhutan, Cambodia, Maldives, Myanmar, Nepal, the Philippines, Seychelles, Sri Lanka, Vietnam, Indonesia and Laos.
Pragati, which stands for Partnership of Regional Armies for Growth and Transformation in the Indian Ocean Region, was designed to provide a platform for participating armies to exchange experiences, share best practices and strengthen military-to-military cooperation.
An Indian Defence Ministry spokesman said the exercise focused on counter-terrorism operations in semi-mountainous and jungle terrain, with participants undergoing a series of lectures, demonstrations and practical training before taking part in the final validation exercise.
Training activities included rock craft, ambush and counter-ambush drills, slithering, jungle lane shooting, room and bus intervention, improvised explosive device detection, casualty evacuation and other specialised military skills.
[[nid:1453106]]
It said mixed teams comprising personnel from different countries trained together throughout the exercise, enhancing interoperability, capacity building and mutual trust among the participating forces.
The spokesman said the maiden edition of Exercise Pragati had laid a strong foundation for future iterations of the exercise while contributing to greater interoperability, collective readiness and mutual trust among participating nations.
[[nid:1453105]]
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Saudi award reflects strength of Malaysia's haj management system
Article:
SEPANG: Malaysia's haj operation this year was highly organised, systematic and well managed, said Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Religious Affairs) Dr Zulkifli Hasan.
He said the prestigious Labaytum Diamond Award 2026 awarded to Lembaga Tabung Haji (TH) was proof that Malaysia has one of the best haj management systems in the world.
"It also proves that Malaysia's haj management system has become a global benchmark," he told reporters upon his return from Jeddah today.
Zulkifli said the Labaytum Diamond Award is the highest recognition presented by the Saudi Haj and Umrah Ministry to countries that demonstrate excellence in haj management and services.
"This is the fifth Labaytum award received by Malaysia and the second time the country has won the highest distinction, the Diamond Award," he said.
Zulkifli attributed the achievement to TH's meticulous planning and execution of haj operations, covering every aspect of the pilgrimage journey, including pre-departure training courses, flights, accommodation, logistics, food services and security arrangements.
"The comprehensive preparations ensured greater comfort and convenience for Malaysian pilgrims throughout their stay in the Holy Land."
Zulkifli also highlighted several innovations, including the Green Haj initiative, which focuses on sustainability through recycling and upcycling programmes involving ihram garments and other reusable materials.
He said Malaysia had also implemented cleanliness campaigns, with Malaysians drawing attention for their cleanliness and organisation.
Zulkifli also expressed appreciation to TH personnel and healthcare workers from the Health Ministry for their dedication in ensuring the welfare and comfort of Malaysian pilgrims.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: After the AI binge, companies balk at soaring bills
Article:
ARTIFICIAL intelligence is getting expensive – and companies are starting to rethink their embrace of the disruptive technology.
Playing by a well-worn Silicon Valley playbook, AI companies charged rock-bottom prices to hook customers after ChatGPT burst onto the scene.
Kevin Simback of startup incubator Delphi Labs calls it the era of "subsidised intelligence" – meaning investors were basically footing the bill so companies could offer AI on the cheap.
"But the tides are beginning to turn," Simback warned and an era where the big AI companies actually need to make money has begun – with leaders OpenAI and Anthropic looking to go public and attract main street investors later this year.
Prices are rising across the board, and one big reason is AI agents.
Unlike a chatbot that just answers questions, agents actually do things – book appointments, write code, manage files. And they're expensive to run, because one task can spin up dozens of agents all working at once, each racking up charges.
Those charges are measured in tokens – the basic unit AI companies use to bill customers. A single agent-powered task can burn through dozens of times' more tokens than a simple chat message.
Meanwhile, the computer chips and data centres needed to power all this AI can't keep up with demand, creating computing shortages and adding further uncertainty to the nascent industry.
"Especially in developer circles, the cost to use AI for things like coding has grown exponentially," said Mark Barton of tech consultancy Omniux. "All the costs are really starting to skyrocket."
Some companies have been so eager to use AI that they've gone overboard in a usage binge called "tokenmaxxing."
"In some cases people are seeing the cost of tokens exceed the cost of the employee within a month or two of use, just because they're using it too much," says analyst Jack Gold of J.Gold Associates.
Even Meta – which earlier this year encouraged employees to use as many tokens as possible as a measure of productivity – has had second thoughts.
"Nobody should be using AI tools just for the sake of using them," chief technology officer Andrew Bosworth wrote in a memo to staff, reported by the Wall Street Journal.
Uber's chief operating officer this week went a step further, raising eyebrows by saying all this AI spending was showing no noticeable increase in productivity.
To cut costs, some companies are switching to free, open-source AI models that anyone can download – not as powerful as ChatGPT or Anthropic's Claude, but good enough for many tasks.
Others are moving to smaller, more specialised models built for specific industries like real estate or finance, rather than giant general-purpose ones.
And some are simply breaking big AI tasks into smaller steps, handing each piece to the cheapest model that can handle it.
The price difference can be dramatic.
"The big large monolithic model, it's US$15 per million tokens, but you can get that down to like five cents if you use the smaller mini model," says Adrian Balfour of consultancy Enverso.
All of this points to AI becoming more like a commodity – where the specific model matters less than finding the right one at the right price.
But don't count out the big players and their state-of-the-art models just yet.
"The most advanced users" will always be willing to pay for the best, says John Belton, a portfolio manager at Gabelli Funds. "It's a growing pie."
* The writer is from AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Philippine senator Estrada surrenders over graft case
Article:
MANILA: A Philippine senator who is the son of a former president surrendered to police on Monday after a court ordered his arrest over a charge that he received illicit payouts as part of an infrastructure scandal that sparked public protests last year.
The anti-graft court ordered the arrest of Senator Jose "Jinggoy" Estrada for plunder, a non-bailable offence under Philippine laws.
The Office of the Ombudsman last week charged Estrada with violating anti-corruption laws for earning "kickbacks" amounting to 573 million pesos (US$9.3 million).
"I will not seek Senate custody," Estrada told reporters at the Senate, referring to the potential for him to seek refuge in the upper house instead of surrendering to police.
"I am ready to defend myself before the court and I will not hide behind the institution to evade the process," he added.
Estrada has denied wrongdoing and said the charges were politically driven. He posted bail last week for a separate offence.
The corruption scandal, which has centred on dangerously flawed flood-control facilities across the Philippines, has shaken the graft-weary nation and slowed economic growth in recent quarters.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: TTDI residents unimpressed with park upgrade, want transparency over plans, outstanding works
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Visitors to the Bukit Kiara Federal Park today were disappointed after a near two-year closure only resulted in visible enhancements about 300m from the entrance.
Residents of Taman Tun Dr Ismail (TTDI) have voiced concerns about the RM6 million channelled into upgrading the park, calling for transparency on the original promised scope and a timeline for completing outstanding works.
"We would appreciate a clear comparison between the original promised scope and what has actually been delivered, along with a timeline for completing any outstanding works," TTDI Residents Association chairman Abu Hafiz Abu Huraira said.
[[nid:1453088]]
While expressing appreciation to the National Landscape Department in upgrading the park, he said the poor condition of the playground, and the lack of amenities such as benches and shaded seating areas did not reflect the scale of the investment.
"The iconic suspension bridge at the top part of the park was supposed to be fixed in 2023 but it's still not fixed.
[[nid:1453087]]
"It appears to be a missed opportunity to deliver a more transformative upgrade for a park that serves many families and joggers daily, taking into account that the project took two years to complete," he said.
Abu Hafiz added that the residents expected proper desilting of the ponds, improved water quality, structural works, and better landscaping when the project was first presented to them.
Residents were also made to understand that the playground and exercise equipment would be modernised, made safer and more attractive for families.
[[nid:1453086]]
The New Straits Times also noticed that the fence of the park facing Jalan Abang Haji Openg was damaged, with a large part still covered in blue metal hoarding.
Signage on the blue hoarding showed that rectification works will only be completed on Sept 22.
Park visitor and TTDI resident Eugene Yong, 45, said the public started entering the park one to two weeks ago with construction debris still scattered around the park.
With only one part of the park beingupgraded, Yong expressed disappointment over the outcome.
"Nothing changed here (on the other side of the park) except maybe they added some of those cement benches. Even the playground is still the same.
"So, I really don't know where the RM6 million went. It really doesn't look like RM6 million.
[[nid:1453085]]
"Even if you compare the pathway with the old one, the new one looks shoddier than this," he said.
He added that another notable upgrade was the repair of the man-made waterfall which previously was not functioning.
However, visitor Syukri Mamat, 34, who was there with his family for the first time since the park's closure for upgrade works in 2024, said that the upgrade to the front part of the park was satisfactory.
"They upgraded the walkway, they repaired the bridge and the rest area.
"It seems safer now. Before this there was a lot of broken equipment.
"But there are still a few things like the playground, like the (outdoor) gym equipment that they didn't repair," he said.
However , a 38-year-old engineer who only wanted to be known as Sailesh said, the duration of the upgrade works, which began on July 8, 2024, did not commensurate with the outcome he saw today.
[[nid:1453084]]
"I'm wondering what took so long for this kind of work... from what I can see of this side of the park (about 300m after the main entrance), there's really nothing done," said Sailesh, who was a frequent visitor to the park before its closure in 2024.
He said that his daughter has seen better playgrounds compared to the current one at the park, though the park was still better than others for jogs due to the spacious walkways.
The NST has made multiple efforts to get comments from Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh but has yet to receive a reply.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Iranian attacks damaged 20 US military sites since war began
Article:
ISTANBUL: Iranian strikes have damaged 20 US military sites since the start of the US-Israeli war against Teheran in February, a BBC analysis of satellite images and videos showed Monday, Anadolu Ajansi (AA) reported.
The targeted facilities span Saudi Arabia, the United Arab Emirates, Qatar, Kuwait, Iraq, Jordan, Bahrain, and Oman, as found in the analysis by BBC Verify using satellite imagery from multiple international providers, along with archived images from Planet, a major provider, to assess damage from Iranian attacks.
While the White House has repeatedly said Iran's military capability has been largely destroyed, analysts argue that damage observed at US facilities suggests that Iran's counterstrikes were both more precise and more extensive than American officials have publicly acknowledged.
Among the significant losses were three advanced anti-ballistic missile battery systems at Al Ruwais and Al Sader airbases in the UAE, as well as Muwaffaq Salti Airbase in Jordan, the report said.
The satellite image analysis also indicated that Iranian strikes severely damaged US refuelling and surveillance aircraft at Prince Sultan Airbase in Saudi Arabia, with wrecked aircraft and burn marks visible at the site.
Among the aircraft identified by a MAIAR analyst was an E-3 Sentry surveillance plane, which US media reports say could cost up to US$700 million to replace.
Elsewhere, Iranian strikes also hit Ali Al Salem Airbase and Camp Arifjan in Kuwait.
Analysts at MAIAR identified destroyed fuel storage bunkers, aircraft hangars, and troop accommodation in satellite imagery, indicating the base was struck multiple times during the conflict.
At Camp Arifjan, defence intelligence firm Janes reported significant damage to satellite communications equipment, the analysis showed.
Tensions in West Asia have escalated since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran on Feb 28 . Teheran retaliated with attacks targeting Israel and US allies in the Gulf, alongside the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.
A ceasefire took effect on April 8 through Pakistani mediation, but subsequent talks in Islamabad failed to produce a lasting agreement.
Since then, the two sides have continued exchanging proposals and counterproposals in an effort to resume direct talks and end the conflict.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Gold falls as renewed Middle East tensions fuel inflation fears
Article:
NEW YORK: Gold prices fell on Monday as renewed US-Iran tensions pushed the dollar and oil prices higher, fuelling fears of inflation and reinforcing the higher-for-longer interest rate outlook.
Spot gold was down 0.8 per cent at US$4,498.89 per ounce at 0909 GMT after hitting a two-week high on Friday. The yellow metal dropped 0.9 per cent in May, its fourth consecutive monthly fall.
US gold futures for August delivery fell 1.4 per cent to US$4,528.90.
The dollar edged higher, making greenback-priced bullion more expensive for holders of other currencies.
The US said it struck Iranian military sites over the weekend and Iran's Revolutionary Guards on Monday said they had targeted a US base in response, the latest exchange of attacks amid negotiations to end the three-month-old war.
"The optimism surrounding negotiations between the US and Iran aimed at ending the standoff in the Strait of Hormuz faded over the weekend," ActivTrades analyst Ricardo Evangelista said. "As a result, energy prices rebounded, reviving inflation concerns and reinforcing hawkish Federal Reserve expectations."
Brent crude oil prices gained more than three per cent after the latest strikes. Higher oil prices can accelerate inflation and keep interest rates higher for longer. While gold is traditionally seen as a hedge against inflation, it loses its appeal in a high-interest-rate environment as a non-yielding asset.
Traders are now pricing in a Fed rate hike this year, with a 40 per cent chance of a quarter-point increase in December, according to CME Group's FedWatch tool.
A host of Fed board members are set to speak this week, while major data releases are scheduled to include the ISM survey of manufacturing and the May payrolls report on Friday.
"Traders will be closely watching this week's key data releases as these have the potential to reshape expectations regarding the future path of Fed monetary policy, influencing demand for the US dollar and, consequently, the performance of gold prices," Evangelista said.
Spot silver rose 0.7 per cent to US$75.79 per ounce, platinum gained 0.4 per cent to US$1,925.26 and palladium fell 0.8 per cent to US$1,343.55.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: China accuses Japan defence minister of 'baseless' claims
Article:
BEIJING: China accused Japan's defence minister on Monday of spreading "baseless" claims and sowing confusion, a day after he took a veiled swipe at Beijing.
Defence Minister Shinjiro Koizumi had pledged on Sunday to keep strengthening Japan's military and warned China was expanding its capabilities without sufficient transparency.
His comments follow a months-long diplomatic spat between the Asian rivals, which began when Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi suggested in November that Japan could intervene militarily if China attempted to seize Taiwan, the self-ruled island claimed by Beijing.
China's foreign ministry said on Monday that Koizumi's comments  were "entirely baseless."
"They appear pale and feeble in the face of a series of historical and legal facts and figures," ministry spokesman Lin Jian told a news briefing.
"This Japanese official deliberately... attempts to turn the tables and sow confusion," Lin said.
"Japan's so-called dialogue is nothing but hypocrisy – a performance put on for appearances, devoid of any genuine sincerity," he added.
Under Takaichi, Japan has quickened its pivot towards a more proactive defence policy, further shaking off – with US encouragement – a pacifist outlook, which has been in place since the end of World War II.
Koizumi made his comments at the annual IISS Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore, saying China's expanding military capabilities were "a matter of serious concern for Japan."
Tokyo would "steadily build up its defence capabilities and make continuous updates with a high degree of transparency", including in the fields of artificial intelligence, uncrewed systems as well as cyber and space defence, he said.
Beijing has frequently rebuked Tokyo for its pivot and said following a reckless policy of "new militarism" that could destabilise the region.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hamzah allies back Tuan Ibrahim's push for Muafakat Nasional revival
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Two allies of Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin, both of whom were sacked from Bersatu, have backed Pas deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man's proposal to revive Muafakat Nasional (MN).
Former Bersatu supreme council member Datuk Seri Saifuddin Abdullah welcomed the proposal, saying it was consistent with Hamzah's vision of building a grand political coalition ahead of the 16th General Election (GE16).
The Indera Mahkota member of Parliament said Hamzah had unveiled the "New National Consensus" framework on June 22 last year, aimed at making Malaysia a more advanced, prosperous and harmonious nation.
"On Feb 14, he launched Reset as a movement to implement the framework.
"So he has already outlined the objective, the framework to achieve it, and the movement to drive it forward," he told the New Straits Times today.
Saifuddin said such aspirations could only be achieved through political power, whether by forming the government or being part of it.
He said in line with the New National Consensus, it would reflect the aspirations of the rakyat while upholding the provisions of the Federal Constitution, Malaysia Agreement 1963, Rukun Negara, and the nation's history and culture.
"That is why discussions have already been held with major stakeholders, including major political forces such as Umno and Pas, as well as representatives of minority communities.
"The idea is to win GE16 with a stable political configuration — winning majority support from the majority and majority support from the minority," he said.
Another former Bersatu leader aligned with Hamzah, Machang MP Wan Ahmad Fayhsal Wan Ahmad Kamal, said he supported efforts to unify the ummah.
"Anything that consolidates Malay voter support, I am more than happy to support," he said briefly.
Yesterday,
Tuan Ibrahim called for the revival of MN
with a fresh line-up that includes leaders from Umno, Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin's "Reset" camp and other Malay-based parties.
Tuan Ibrahim, who is also Perikatan Nasional deputy chairman, said he believed that reviving the alliance, which he dubbed 'MN 2.0', could help to realise such efforts.
He said that this time, MN should be expanded to include the "Reset" group led by Hamzah's faction, Umno, as well as other parties such as Pejuang, Berjasa, Iman and Perkasa.
MN began as a political cooperation pact in 2019 between Umno and Pas to unite Malay voters against the then-ruling Pakatan Harapan coalition.
The alliance was dissolved in 2022 following ideological clashes and after Umno joined the unity government.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor assembly dissolved, election within 60 days [WATCH]
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The Johor state assembly has been dissolved, paving the way for the next state election to be held within 60 days.
Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said following an audience earlier today, Johor Regent Tunku Ismail Sultan Ibrahim consented to the dissolution of the assembly.
"The 56-seat Johor state assembly is officially dissolved today, paving the way for the 16th Johor state election," he said during a
special press conference
at his official residence in Saujana this afternoon.
Also present were former Johor executive council members and Johor Assembly Speaker Datuk Mohd Puad Zarkashi, among others.
The move ends weeks of speculation, although the assembly's term was due to expire only in April next year.
The 16th Johor state election is shaping up to be dominated by four-cornered contests, with Barisan Nasional (BN), Pakatan Harapan (PH), Perikatan Nasional (PN) and Bersama, all signalling their intention to contest the 56 state seats.
It is expected there will be multi-cornered fights in most constituencies, although several seats could see more than four-cornered contests if smaller parties and independents enter the fray.
The election is also being closely watched as a key test of support for the BN-led Johor state government, the unity government and the strength of opposition forces ahead of the next general election.
In the 2022 state election, Barisan Nasional secured a commanding victory by winning 40 of the 56 seats.
Pakatan Harapan holds 12 seats, Perikatan Nasional three, and Muda one.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Auto dealers expect new hire-purchase rules to boost buyer interest
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The implementation of the reducing balance method and effective interest rate system under the Hire-Purchase (Amendment) Act 2026 is expected to boost business, say second-hand car dealers and potential buyers.
Although foot traffic at showrooms was slow on the first day of the new rules, which coincided with the birthday of His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, sales representatives are confident the changes will spur interest in vehicle hire-purchase financing.
Car salesman Aviss Thung said the new method, where interest is calculated based on the outstanding principal instead of the original loan amount, would make financing easier to explain to customers.
"It is generally good for business. When there is an option to reduce the overall cost, customers will naturally be more interested.
"It also makes it easier for us to market and explain financing packages, especially when savings from early settlement can be significant," he said when met at his dealership near Batu Caves.
He said buyers could pay less in total interest if they settled their loans earlier, but noted it was still too early to gauge the impact on sales as consumers were still trying to understand the changes.
"For now, with several public holidays this week, many people are out of town. But once they return and understand the new rules, I expect more interest in car purchases," he said.
Another salesman, Tan Kai Che, said the changes were likely to have a greater impact on higher-end models, where potential savings from early settlement are more significant.
"This is especially the case for luxury cars. For vehicles below RM100,000, the savings may not be as much.
"There have also been cases where buyers delayed their hire-purchase agreements as they were waiting for this new rule to take effect," he said.
Tan added that sales were expected to pick up once consumers became more familiar with the new financing framework.
Yesterday, Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said the
Hire-Purchase
(Amendment) Act 2026 came into force today as part of efforts to strengthen consumer protection and ensure a fairer, more transparent and competitive financing system.
Automotive associations have described the new law as a positive step, as it gradually replaces the long-standing flat rate and Rule of 78 methods with the internationally recognised reducing balance and effective interest rate system.
Potential buyers were also seen weighing their options following the changes.
Hazahlan Hassan, 65, who visited a showroom to buy a car for his son, said the new method had prompted him to consider purchasing one for himself.
"As a consumer, I think this is a good move. Many buyers change cars after four or five years, so this method is more practical.
"It is still new, so I want to see feedback from others first. But so far, it sounds positive.
"Under the previous method, if we wanted to settle early or switch cars, we could still end up paying a lot in interest. This new method seems fairer because the interest is based on the remaining balance," he said.
Similarly, Hazry Hashim, 57, who was surveying cars, said the changes had piqued his interest.
"Hire purchase can be a burden, but if there is an option to reduce the total cost, it becomes more attractive," he said.
kw Auto, dealers, hire-purchase, cost, interest, Hire-Purchase (Amendment) Act, reduce, pay, traffic, showroom, Malaysia, news, NST
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Defence minister: Norway needs to be held responsible for missile export cancellation, Malaysia’s right to ask for compensation
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 1 — Malaysia has the right to claim appropriate compensation from Norway and the company involved, following the cancellation of the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) export licence, said Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin.
He said the claim is justified given that Malaysia has suffered financial losses, as well as lost valuable time and opportunity as a result of the cancellation.
“A substantial amount of public funds was allocated for this programme. If Norway had informed us earlier about the export licence cancellation, those funds could have been redirected to other purposes,” he said in a statement today.
Mohamed Khaled made these remarks in response to a New Straits Times article today titled “Move on from missile deal and find replacement”.
He said Malaysia is indeed moving forward and actively exploring alternative solutions to meet the nation’s defence needs; however, actions taken without accountability cannot be accepted or simply overlooked.
He stressed that finding a replacement for the NSM system is no easy task, as a weapon of such sophistication cannot be swapped out overnight.
He said the process would require fresh contract negotiations and the integration of a new system into existing platforms, all of which demand considerable time, cost and resources.
“More critically, this episode transcends a bilateral contract dispute. It has left our national defence and security in a vulnerable position.
“A firm message must be sent to Norway and the international defence community: such actions will not go unanswered or unaccounted for,” he said.
He added that Norway must be held accountable in a meaningful way, as both a lesson and a deterrent, to ensure this does not happen again, rather than being let off without consequence.
“What is equally concerning is the growing tendency for commitments and contracts to be set aside based on political or ideological considerations, thereby undermining trust in international agreements,” he said.
Describing Norway’s action as unjustified and damaging to bilateral ties, Mohamed Khaled said smaller nations and middle powers rely on a stable and predictable international environment, where every commitment and obligation is honoured.
“If a country or company can walk away from commitments without any accountability, it sets a dangerous precedent for defence, economic and strategic cooperation in the future. Therefore, compensation is not merely about recovering money that has been spent.
“If Norway truly respects the principles of a rules-based international order and values its long-standing relationship with Malaysia, then it should support a fair and reasonable compensation process, one that takes into account the full impact of this cancellation,” he said. — Bernama

Title: Johor Pakatan says prepared for state polls following dissolution of state assembly
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, June 1 — Johor Pakatan Harapan (PH) is prepared to face the upcoming state election following the dissolution of the state legislative assembly.
In a joint statement, Johor PH said the coalition had expected that Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) would dissolve the assembly soon to pave way for an election.
“From the start, PH expected BN to dissolve the state assembly in the near future.
“In the spirit of democracy, PH will continue with existing preparations to face this election,” read the statement that was released tonight.
The statement was co-signed by Johor PH chairman and Johor Parti Amanah Negara (Amanah) chief Aminolhuda Hassan; Johor PKR chief Datuk Seri Dr Zaliha Mustafa; and Johor DAP chief Teo Nie Ching.
PH Johor also announced that it will hold discussions at the coalition’s Presidential Council level at the national level to plan its next steps.
“For us, there is no easy election. PH is prepared to make the best effort possible to win this state election,” read the statement.
In a related note, the coalition also criticised the June 22 special one-day state legislative assembly sitting, describing it as illogical.
The notice for the June 22 state legislative assembly sitting was widely expected to announce the dissolution of the Johor state assembly.
Earlier, Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi announced the dissolution of the state legislative assembly.
This effectively paves the way for the state’s upcoming election.
https://www.facebook.com/pakatanharapan.my/posts/kenyataan-media-pakatan-harapan-johor-1-jun-2026pembubaran-dun-johor1-pakatan-ha/1333322885565884/

Title: Thunderstorms, heavy rain alert issued for six states until 9pm
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 1 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds expected to affect several areas in six states until 9 pm today.
In a statement, MetMalaysia said the adverse weather is forecast to occur on the east coast, involving the districts of Besut, Setiu and Dungun in Terengganu, as well as Bera and Rompin in Pahang.
Similar conditions are also expected in Jempol, Negeri Sembilan, and in Johor, involving Segamat and Mersing.
Meanwhile, in Sabah, the warning covers the Interior division (Nabawan) and Sandakan division (Telupid, Beluran and Sandakan).
In Sarawak, the affected areas are Kuching, Serian, Samarahan, Sri Aman, Betong (Betong), Sibu (Kanowit), Kapit (Song and Bukit Mabong), Miri (Beluru and Telang Usan), and Limbang. — Bernama

Title: PM Anwar expected at Sarawak state-level Gawai Day celebration on June 21
Article:
KUCHING, June 1 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is expected to attend this year’s Sarawak state-level Gawai Day celebrations to be held here on June 21, said Sarawak Deputy Premier Datuk Amar Douglas Uggah Embas.
He added that the Yang Dipertua Negeri of Sarawak Tun Dr Wan Junaidi Tuanku Jaafar will be the guest of honour at the event, which is also expected to be attended by Sarawak Premier Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg and Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadilah Yusof.
“We will hold the Niti Daun (parade) in Kuching on June 20, while on June 21 the highlight of the state-level Gawai Day celebration will be a gala dinner attended by distinguished guests,” he said.
He told reporters this at the 2026 Gawai Dayak Day Open House at the Unit for Other Religions (UNIFOR) Complex here today.
According to him, Gawai Dayak continues to serve as an important platform for showcasing the culture, traditions and heritage of the Dayak community to people of all races.
“The tradition of ‘ngabang’ (visiting one another), which is practised by the people of Sarawak, symbolises unity, as every celebration is observed together regardless of race or religion,” he said.
At the same time, Uggah described the response to the Gawai Dayak Open House held today as encouraging, with attendance believed to have exceeded the initial target of 2,000 visitors. — Bernama

Title: Police on the trail of three suspects involved in armed robbery cases in Pekan Gurney
Article:
IPOH, June 1 — Police are hunting for three men, believed to be Indonesian nationals, involved in a gang robbery armed with machetes at a house in the Pekan Gurney area, Manjung yesterday.
Manjung District Police Chief, ACP Hasbullah Abd Rahman, said that his department received a report regarding the incident from a female victim in her 40s at 6.27am.
“In the incident, three male suspects, believed to be Indonesian nationals, entered the house and threatened the victim with a machete before fleeing with cash and jewelry. No injuries were reported, and the estimated loss is said to be RM10,000,” he said in a statement today.
He added that there have been four previous incidents with the same modus operandi occurring around Ayer Tawar and Pekan Gurney this year.
“The police are actively conducting investigations to track down and arrest the suspects involved. Members of the public who have any information related to this incident are urged to come forward and assist in the investigation by contacting the Manjung District Police Headquarters operations room at 05-688 6222, the WhatsApp hotline at 017-682 8005, or any nearby police station,” he said.
He stated that the case is being investigated under Section 395/397 of the Penal Code for armed gang robbery. — Bernama

Title: Johor DAP: Marina Ibrahim not offered GLC post, but position with statutory body involved in welfare
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, June 1 — Johor DAP has clarified that the party had offered to appoint incumbent Skudai assemblyman Marina Ibrahim to helm a statutory body and not a government-linked company (GLC) as seen in a letter that went viral.
Johor DAP vice-chairman Sheikh Umar Bagharib Ali said there is a difference in a statutory body and a GLC.
He said Johor DAP chairman Teo Nie Ching had stated that she offered Marina to lead a statutory body and not a GLC.
“Statutory bodies are established by an Act of Parliament or state enactment to fulfill specific public policy or socio-economic objectives, while a GLC is incorporated under the Companies Act 2016 and the government acts as a majority shareholder.
“In this recent issue, Teo had offered Marina to helm a statutory body that is related to social welfare work.
“This is actually a good fit for Marina who has been passionate and known for her welfare activism over the years.,” he said when contacted by
Malay Mail
today.
The DAP’s Central Executive Committee (CEC) member was responding to criticism that the party is allegedly using positions in GLCs as political tools.
Sheikh Umar said the unnamed statutory body was a right fit for Marina based on her work in welfare activism.
“In fact, the party’s leadership felt Marina would be able to perform well and assist in shaping policy by being appointed to the statutory body.
“The proposed appointment by DAP is not a form of reward that is used to lure or motivate someone to perform a task,” he said, adding that it is normal for DAP to initiate moves that are strategic for the party’s long term.
Meanwhile, Johor Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman Aminolhuda Hassan said that such appointments, especially to statutory bodies, was a normal practice for any political parties that are part of the government.
The Johor Parti Amanah Negara (Amanah) chief also clarified that for such appointments only eligible and hardworking party members who have promise will be considered.
“Generally, the person being considered will normally be a subject matter expert or have a good knowledge of the field that the statutory body is established under,” he said, adding that statutory bodies, unlike a GLC, are under the purview of the government and not a corporate company.

Title: Five hurt in five‑vehicle collision in Kudat
Article:
KUDAT, June 1 — Five people were injured in a road accident involving five vehicles at Jalan Kampung Kandawayon here late yesterday.
Kudat Fire and Rescue Station chief Darikson Galanang said the crash involved a Perodua Axia, a Proton, a Toyota Revo and two Perodua Myvi cars.
Three victims travelling in the Axia, comprising two men aged 24 and 33 and a 49-year-old woman, sustained injuries.
Two other injured victims, aged 24 and 26, were the driver and passenger of a grey Myvi.
The drivers of the Toyota Revo, Proton and blue Myvi escaped unhurt.
All five injured victims were taken to hospital for treatment, while the operation concluded at 2.20am. — Daily Express

Title: Johor state assembly dissolved, Onn Hafiz cites need for mandate to drive development
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, June 1 — The Johor state legislative assembly has been dissolved, paving the way for the state’s upcoming election.
Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi announced the dissolution after obtaining the consent of Johor Regent Tunku Mahkota Ismail Sultan Ibrahim earlier today.
He said the move would allow the people of Johor to elect a new state government and secure a mandate to continue the state’s development agenda.
“The Election Commission will ensure the necessary steps to determine the dates for nomination, early voting and polling for the 16th Johor state election,” he said in a brief special media conference held at the Johor Menteri Besar’s official residence in Saujana here today.
Onn Hafiz, who is also the Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman and state Umno chief, was accompanied by the state legislative assembly Speaker Datuk Mohd Puad Zarkashi.
In attendance was also the incumbent ruling assemblyman during the announcement.
The Johor state legislative assembly, which has 56 seats, is scheduled to end its term on April 21 next year.
Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi announced the dissolution after obtaining the consent of Johor Regent Tunku Mahkota Ismail Sultan Ibrahim earlier today. — Bernama pic
Under the provisions of the legislation, the EC is given up to 60 days after the dissolution of the state assembly to hold the election.
The previous Johor state election was held in March 2022. At that time, BN, led by its lynchpin party Umno, formed the state government after winning a supermajority of 40 of the 56 contested state assembly seats.
Pakatan Harapan (PH) won 12 seats with DAP obtaining 10 seats, while PKR and Parti Amanah Negara (Amanah) won one seat each.
Perikatan Nasional (PN) won three seats, while the Malaysian United Democratic Alliance (Muda) obtained one seat.

Title: Man missing, feared drowned after boat overturns near SK Nanga Balang in Kapit
Article:
KANOWIT, June 1 — A 32-year-old man is feared drowned after a boat carrying seven passengers capsized near SK Nanga Balang in Gaat, Kapit, this morning.
The victim, identified as Spielberg Dylan Sekunyir, was reported missing following the incident, while six other passengers managed to reach safety.
According to the Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department (Bomba), an emergency call regarding the incident was received at 9.28am.
A spokesperson for the department said firefighters from the Kapit Fire and Rescue Station (BBP Kapit) were dispatched to the scene after first proceeding to the Kapit police station to obtain information on the incident.
“The boat was reported to have capsized with seven people on board. Six passengers were rescued safely, while one male passenger, aged 32, remains missing and has yet to be found,” the spokesperson said.
A team comprising three firefighters travelled to the location in a Bomba utility vehicle.
As of the latest update, the victim had not been located and search efforts were being coordinated. — The Borneo Post

Title: Public rescue injured driver, passenger after crash near SMK Sematan, says Sarawak Fire Dept
Article:
KUCHING, June 1 — A man was seriously injured in a road accident involving a sedan in front of Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan (SMK) Sematan yesterday afternoon.
The Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department (Bomba) Operations Centre said the injured man was the driver of the vehicle involved in the accident.
“The incident was reported at 1.22pm. The male driver was not trapped but sustained serious injuries, while a female passenger also suffered injuries.
“Both victims had been extricated by members of the public before firefighters arrived at the scene,” it said in a statement.
The statement added that the Bomba operations team placed the injured driver on a stretcher before handing him over to Ministry of Health (MOH) personnel for further treatment.
The female passenger also received medical attention following the incident. — The Borneo Post

Title: Family among four killed in Toyota Vios as BMW, Mercedes, Wira and Alza collide near Simpang Renggam Prison
Article:
KLUANG, June 1 — Four people were killed and two others injured in a collision involving five vehicles along Jalan Renggam–Simpang Renggam, in front of Simpang Renggam Prison, this afternoon.
